text: My absolute go-to after long dive days. The ahi poke bowl is the best on the island — fresh, perfectly seasoned, and they never skimp on the fish. Locally owned and they clearly care about sourcing responsibly.
Best late-night poke in Kailua. DoorDash delivers fast and it always arrives fresh. The garlic shrimp plate is incredible.
Solid seafood, great local vibe. The mahi plate is generous and the staff is always friendly.
My weekend açaí bowl ritual. The açaí is thick and not too sweet, granola is housemade, and the cold brew is excellent. Perfect way to start a paddle morning.
Great spot for a light breakfast before heading to the beach. Staff is warm and the avocado toast is legitimately good.
Sam and I came here for our anniversary and it was perfect. The fish special was gorgeous and the wine list had some real surprises. Quiet enough to actually have a conversation.
Very good food, clearly seasonal and local. Service was attentive without being intrusive. Prices are fair for what you get.
Aunty Lani is a Kailua treasure. The kalua pork plate is everything — smoky, tender, the real deal. Cash only but worth it every time.
Classic plate lunch done right. Fast, affordable, and always fresh. The mac salad is creamy perfection.

This shop is the reason my dive gear is always in top shape. Kaipo and the team at Windward are thorough, knowledgeable, and genuinely passionate about the reef. Highly recommend their gear inspection service.
Got my BCD serviced here. Fast turnaround, fair price, and they noticed a small issue with my regulator I hadn't caught. Great shop.
I order their single-origin beans every couple months and they're consistently phenomenal. The Kona natural process is rich and complex. So glad there's a local roaster this good right in Kailua.
Great coffee shop for remote work. They have oat milk and know how to pull a shot properly. Cozy atmosphere.
The spicy octopus bowl is legitimately the best tako poke I've had. Fresh daily. Fast line. Local owned. Five stars, no question.
Great poke, good value. The mixed seafood bowl is generous. Good option when Tide Pool Kitchen is too busy.
Saturday morning ritual — herbs for the lanai garden, local honey, and fresh papaya. Farmers are friendly and prices are fair. Love supporting this community market.
Good selection of local produce and crafts. Gets busy on Saturdays. Come early for the best picks.
Solid Thai kitchen in Kailua — the drunken noodles with shrimp are great and they happily accommodate pescatarian requests. Good for a weeknight when you want something flavorful and not poke.
Reliable and tasty. The green curry is fragrant and not too spicy. Generous portions for the price.
The freshest fish on the windward side, hands down. They know the boats, they know the seasons. I always stop here before a home cook night after field work.
Best fish market in Kaneohe. Always fresh, the staff knows their fish, and they'll recommend how to cook whatever you're buying.
Good selection of local and organic produce. I pick up reef-safe sunscreen and kombucha here regularly. A bit pricier than a regular grocery but worth supporting.
The lilikoi sorbet is magic. Perfect post-beach treat with Jenna. They use local fruit and it shows — clean, bright flavor. A Kailua must-stop.
I love that they use locally caught fish and publish the source on the board. The lomi salmon bowl is a beautiful nod to Hawaiian tradition. Would eat here every week.
Rented gear here for a day trip. Everything was clean and well-maintained. Great service and they gave good tips on local dive spots.
